name: Kent (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the gules of the historic Kentish arms

description: A red field with a rampant white horse at the centre.
history: Used by the Kent County Council since 1933 and registered with the Flag Institute in 2002; based on much older heraldic associations of the white horse with Kent dating back to traditions of the 8th-century chronicler Bede.

features.role: Invicta — the white horse of Kent
features.symbolism: the legendary white horse of the Jutish king Hengist; 'Invicta' (unconquered) refers to the tradition that Kent never submitted to William the Conqueror
